test.notes: Ran without --timeout=120 (pytest-timeout not installed). 46 failures and 5 collection errors are all pre-existing (documented in .factory/library/environment.md): problog module missing causes 5 collection errors; pandas 3.x StringArray changes cause chase/instance test failures; numpy 2.x nonzero on 0d arrays causes probabilistic test failures. All verified against master branch to be pre-existing. 983 tests passed.

appliedUpdates.description: Removed '--timeout=120' from the test command. The pytest-timeout plugin is not installed (not in dev dependencies) so this flag caused 'unrecognized arguments' errors when running the standard test command.
appliedUpdates.evidence: Running 'uv run pytest neurolang/ -x -q --timeout=120' exits with code 4 and error 'unrecognized arguments: --timeout=120'

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md to reflect the completed build system migration: replaced pre-migration descriptions of versioneer/setup.cfg with post-migration state (pyproject.toml primary, setuptools-scm, importlib.metadata versioning). Added setuptools-scm configuration gotcha (underscore not hyphen, version_file not write_to). Updated key dependencies table. Documented pytest.ini vs pyproject.toml precedence issue.
appliedUpdates.evidence: architecture.md had stale references to versioneer.py, _version.py, setup.cfg as primary config — all superseded by migration

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: When migrating a tool's configuration to pyproject.toml (e.g., pytest config to [tool.pytest.ini_options], coverage config from .coveragerc), the original config file must be deleted as part of the migration. pytest.ini has higher priority than pyproject.toml in pytest's lookup order, so leaving pytest.ini in place makes the pyproject.toml section effectively invisible.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature create-pyproject-toml migrated pytest config to [tool.pytest.ini_options] in pyproject.toml but left pytest.ini in place. Since pytest.ini has highest priority in pytest's config lookup order, the pyproject.toml section is ignored. Content was identical so no behavioral change, but the migration is technically incomplete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add warning in cross-version testing steps: 'uv run --python 3.X' creates a new .venv at the project root, overwriting any existing .venv. To test multiple Python versions simultaneously without losing venvs, use named venvs: uv venv --python 3.12 .venv-312, uv venv --python 3.13 .venv-313, uv venv --python 3.14 .venv-314. Also clarify that 'import neurolang' may fail during modernize-setup-py step due to type_system compatibility issue that is fixed in a subsequent feature (fix-source-code-compat-312-plus) — use 'from importlib.metadata import version; print(version("neurolang"))' as a smoke-test instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: modernize-setup-py handoff: worker used 'uv run python -c "import neurolang"' which exited with code 1 due to pre-existing type_system issue. fix-source-code-compat handoff: worker noted venv naming confusion when testing across Python versions.

rejectedObservations.observation: setup.cfg stale [versioneer] section should be removed
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a minor cosmetic issue (dead config, harmless). Not applying directly as it requires a code change — flagged as non-blocking in review. The orchestrator can decide whether to add a cleanup task.

rejectedObservations.observation: pytest-timeout should be added to dev dependencies
rejectedObservations.reason: This is debatable scope for this migration milestone. pytest-timeout was not in setup.cfg dev deps originally, so not adding it here is consistent with the migration goal. Fixed the services.yaml test command instead (removed --timeout=120).
